steps.title: Identify the angle sum property
steps.content: The sum of all interior angles in any triangle equals 180°. We know ∠K = 34° and ∠L = 34°, so we set up an equation to find ∠J.

steps.title: Apply the angle sum formula and solve
steps.content:
The sum of angles in triangle JKL:

$$\angle J + \angle K + \angle L = 180^\circ$$

Substitute the known angles:

$$\angle J + 34^\circ + 34^\circ = 180^\circ$$

Combine like terms:

$$\angle J + 68^\circ = 180^\circ$$

Subtract 68° from both sides:

$$\angle J = 180^\circ - 68^\circ = 112^\circ$$

The measure of ∠J is 112.
